# PACK&GO Refactor Plan — 2026-05-18

**Triggered by:** Vibe Coding workflow Stage 2 (CLAUDE.md §九)
**Input:** docs/refactor/audit-2026-05-18.md (commit 4ef11ca)
**Decisions confirmed by Jeff:** see "Decision Log" below
**Generated by:** Plan subagent (Stage 2 finalizer)
**Working tree at plan time:** 227 uncommitted files (must stabilize in Phase 0)

---

## Decision Log

| Q | Decision | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Q0 | Commit/stash WIP first (Phase 0) | 227 uncommitted files. Multi-week refactor on unsynced tree = 改 A 壞 B risk. Must reach a clean HEAD before any phase starts. |
| Q1 | Burst mode (~30h week 1-2, sparse after) | Jeff's actual schedule. Plan front-loads risky/blocking work in week 1, cleanup + verification in week 2. |
| Q2 | 5 grouped routers PRs by risk tier | Balance landing speed (safe domains ship fast) against money-path safety (Stripe paths get solo review). |
| Q3 | tsc → stripe → routers | Money-path safety > organizational tidiness. Stripe transactions + tests must land BEFORE routers split touches `bookings`/`vouchers`/`packpoint`/`accounting`. |
| Q4 | Defer P1-3, most P1-5, P1-9 | Out of 2-week scope. Saves ~50-70h. Keeps in-scope only what's needed for tsc green + money safety + routers split + critical dead-code purge. |
| Q5 | Weekday-morning risky deploys, no feature flags | Refactor must be behaviorally identical. Vitest happy-path tests + money-path rigorous tests are the regression anchor. Risky phases (stripe, db, routers, money) ship Tue-Thu morning so Jeff is alert for any rollback. |
| Q6 | Money 100%, non-money happy-path | Pragmatic for solo shop. Money paths (stripe webhook, refunds, voucher consume, packpoint redeem): happy + failure + idempotent retry. Everything else: 1 happy-path Vitest per touched module as regression anchor. |

---

## Strategy

The refactor is sequenced by **risk-compounding safety**: each phase makes the next safer. Phase 0 stabilizes the working tree so no phase ships on top of unrelated WIP. Phase 1 (tsc green) unblocks the "tsc must pass" rule and exposes hidden schema drift before it gets buried under restructuring. Phase 2 (Stripe webhook tests + transactions) protects revenue BEFORE Phase 4 splits the routers that consume those flows. Phase 3 (dead code purge) reduces surface area before structural changes. Phase 4 (routers.ts → 5 sub-PRs grouped by risk tier) is the biggest unlock, sequenced safe-domains-first so we land confidence before touching money paths. Phase 5 covers selected P1s that have tsc errors or are blockers (ToursTab, AutonomousAgentsTab, supplierSyncService). Phase 6 = final verification, smoke tests, and docs update.

Deferred to v2: TourDetailPeony split (P1-3, 10-14h), most other 1000+ LOC client splits (P1-5 partial), the 116 hard-coded Chinese strings sweep (P1-9), masterAgent.ts split (P0-4, 16-24h), full autonomous-agents reshape (P0-5 split work, ~20-30h), db.ts split (P1-4, 8-12h). These are real but they each demand their own Vibe Coding cycle and don't block daily ops. They go on the v2 backlog.

Total in-scope effort: **~62-86h AI / ~12-16h Jeff review**, fits the burst-mode budget if Jeff can clear 2 weeks of evenings + a couple of weekend afternoons.

---

## Phase 0 · WIP Stabilization

**Audit items covered:** N/A (prerequisite — protects every phase below)
**Time est:** 2-4h AI / 1-2h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Day 0 (before anything else)

**Goal:** Reach a clean `git status` at HEAD so subsequent phases don't tangle with unrelated WIP.

**Deliverables:**
- Coherent commits of mature WIP files (grouped by feature/concern)
- Stash unfinished/exploratory work (named stashes with descriptions)
- Confirmed-deletes for files marked `D` that were already meant to be removed (e.g., AIAdvisor.tsx, Destinations.tsx, Hero.tsx, ManusDialog.tsx, PriceDisplay.tsx, EditableHero.tsx, FeaturedTours.tsx, HomeHeroSpotlight.tsx — these match the Round 80 cleanup pattern)
- Clean working tree at HEAD verified by `git status --short` returning empty

**Risk:** Low (no behavior change — git hygiene only).

**Verification gate:**
- `git status --short` returns 0 lines
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` baseline captured: expect ~40 errors (same as Stage 1 audit; not improved yet, but not worsened)
- `pnpm test` baseline captured: expect existing pass count (regression anchor for later phases)

**Sub-agent strategy (per CLAUDE.md §9.4):**
- **Supervisor:** Reads `git status --porcelain` + recent commits, builds a triage table grouping files by inferred concern (admin UI cleanup / round-80 deletions / Dockerfile / cookie banner / footer / etc.).
- **Sub-agents (parallel, isolated context):**
  - Agent A: Round-80 deletion confirmations (validate each `D` file is truly unreferenced) → produces deletion commit
  - Agent B: Modified-but-coherent client UI files (Header, Footer, NewsletterSection, etc.) → produces UI tweak commit
  - Agent C: Admin UI WIP (DashboardTab, ToursTab, MonitorDashboard, etc.) → either commit (if stable) or stash (if exploratory)
  - Agent D: Server/infra (Dockerfile, robots.txt, anything in server/) → produces infra commit
- **Coordination:** Each sub-agent works on a disjoint file set; supervisor approves the staging before commit. Jeff reviews each commit message before push.

**Rollback:** Soft-reset HEAD if a commit was wrong (commits remain in reflog 90d). Stashes are recoverable via `git stash list`.

**Manual intervention flag:** Jeff must personally read each proposed commit message before push. AI cannot judge whether an in-progress UI tweak is "done enough to commit" vs "stash for next session" — that's a Jeff call.

---

## Phase 1 · tsc Error Cleanup (40 errors → 0)

**Audit items covered:** P0-3 (40 errors across 16 files)
**Time est:** 4-6h AI / 1h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Day 1 (week 1, morning) — fresh context, single focused session per CLAUDE.md §九 §9.1

**Goal:** `pnpm tsc --noEmit` returns exit code 0, unblocking the "tsc must pass" red-line.

**Approach (sequential within the phase):**
1. **First:** Fix tsconfig `target` / `downlevelIteration` — single config change closes ~10 errors (calibrationAgent, selfRetrospective, lionTravelApiService, yearEndExportService Set/Map iteration errors).
2. **Then:** Schema-drift errors in three clusters (parallelizable across sub-agents):
   - **Cluster A — agents/autonomous/**: agentTools.ts:291,297,299,301,302 (`bookings.status/adults/contactEmail/contactName`), opsAgent.ts:273 (`tours.days`), opsAgent.ts:411 (`InvokeResult.content`), opsActions.ts:316 (`bookings.notes`), selfRetrospective.ts:219-224 (4 implicit-any). Decide per case: schema-missing or code-stale.
   - **Cluster B — routers.ts**: 2453 (`db.listTours`), 2454, 2508, 2532, 4190 (`tour.priceUsd`), 5219, 5912, 5927, 7902.
   - **Cluster C — services + admin**: stripeWebhook.ts:824 (`"emergency"` inquiry-category), supplierSyncService.ts:224,511 (string→Date), tourMapGenerator.ts, yearEndExportService.ts, ToursTab.tsx, AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx, requireAdmin.ts, marketingWorker.ts, contentAnalyzerAgent.ts.
3. **Then:** Add pre-commit `tsc --noEmit` hook in husky (one-line, prevents regression).

**Sub-agent strategy:**
- **Supervisor:** Holds the error inventory, dispatches one cluster per sub-agent, gates merge on each cluster's tsc-clean diff.
- **Sub-agents (3 in parallel):**
  - Sub-A: Cluster A (autonomous agents tsc fixes only) — must NOT split/restructure files, just resolve type drift
  - Sub-B: Cluster B (routers.ts tsc fixes only) — same constraint
  - Sub-C: Cluster C (services + admin) — same constraint
- **Coordination:** Sub-agents do NOT cross file boundaries. If a fix needs a schema change (drizzle/schema.ts), they pause and escalate to supervisor; supervisor decides + applies schema delta itself (single owner of schema.ts during this phase).

**Verification gate:**
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0 (zero errors)
- `pnpm test` regression-anchor pass count unchanged
- Husky pre-commit hook present and triggers on commit attempt

**Rollback:** Each cluster lands as its own commit; revert the offending cluster commit if any cluster introduces runtime regression.

**Deploy:** None — type-only fixes, no behavior change. (If a schema migration was needed, that ships with its own deploy in Phase 2's window.)

**Vitest plan:** No new tests required (type fixes are mechanical). EXCEPTION: if a schema-drift fix changes runtime behavior (e.g., a previously-crashing path now succeeds with new typed shape), add a 1-line happy-path test as regression anchor.

---

## Phase 2 · Stripe Webhook Hardening (tests + transactions)

**Audit items covered:** P0-2 (stripeWebhook.ts: money path, 0 tests, no DB transactions)
**Time est:** 8-10h AI / 2h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Days 2-3 (week 1) — Tuesday or Wednesday morning deploy

**Goal:** Money path is transactionally safe, centrally idempotent, and covered by rigorous Vitest (happy + failure + idempotent-retry per Q6).

**Approach (sequential):**
1. **Add central idempotency table:** New Drizzle table `stripeWebhookEvents` keyed on `event.id` with status enum. Single up-front check at handler entry; remove per-handler `if (existing)` checks (lines 176, 227, 260, 484, 747, 929).
2. **Wrap multi-write handlers in `db.transaction(...)`:** Every handler that does ≥2 writes (the booking-update + packpoint-award sequence at lines 218/260 is the canonical case). Estimated 10-12 handlers need wrapping.
3. **Fix line 824 type error** if not already cleared in Phase 1 (inquiry-category union).
4. **Add Vitest coverage (money-path 100% per Q6):**
   - `charge.succeeded` booking path: happy + duplicate-event (idempotent retry) + DB-fail-mid-handler (transaction rollback)
   - `payment_intent.succeeded` booking path: same 3 cases
   - `payment_intent.failed`: status update + customer email scheduling
   - `charge.refunded` booking path: happy + voucher-restore + packpoint-restore + idempotent retry
   - `customer.subscription.*` membership lifecycle: happy + idempotent
   - `payment_intent.succeeded` visa path: happy + idempotent
   - Use mocked Stripe events; do NOT hit real DB (use Drizzle's in-memory test setup or per-test transaction-rollback).
5. **DEFER the file split** (audit suggested splitting into `stripeWebhook/{bookings,visa,subscriptions,refunds,index}.ts` if still >300 LOC). After transactions + central idempotency lands, re-measure. If still >300 LOC, do split as Phase 2B; otherwise defer to v2.

**Sub-agent strategy:**
- **Supervisor:** Holds the handler inventory, sequences transaction-wrapping and test-writing, gates each handler's commit.
- **Sub-agents (parallel after central idempotency lands):**
  - Sub-A: Booking handlers (charge.succeeded + payment_intent.succeeded + payment_intent.failed) → wrap + tests
  - Sub-B: Refund handlers (charge.refunded) → wrap + tests
  - Sub-C: Subscription handlers (customer.subscription.*) → wrap + tests
  - Sub-D: Visa handlers → wrap + tests
- **Coordination:** Central idempotency table + migration lands FIRST as a single supervisor commit (sub-agents can't proceed until the table exists). Then sub-agents work on disjoint handler sets in parallel.

**Verification gate:**
- `pnpm test server/_core/stripeWebhook.test.ts` — all new tests pass (estimated 18-24 test cases)
- `pnpm test` — full suite still green
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0
- Manual smoke (Jeff): replay a known Stripe event via `stripe trigger payment_intent.succeeded` against staging — verify booking + packpoint + email all happen, and replaying the same event is a no-op.

**Rollback:** Phase 2 lands as 3-4 commits (idempotency table + migration; transactions per handler family; tests). Each independently revertable. If revert needed mid-production, Stripe events will simply retry (Stripe's own retry policy) — no data loss as long as the central idempotency table is preserved.

**Deploy:** Weekday morning (Tue/Wed/Thu 9-11am PT). Watch Stripe dashboard for 30min post-deploy. Have rollback commit ready.

**Vitest plan (rigorous per Q6):**
- `server/_core/stripeWebhook.test.ts` — happy/failure/idempotent for each of 6 main handler paths
- `server/_core/stripeWebhookIdempotency.test.ts` — central dedupe table behavior
- Module size targets: `stripeWebhook.ts` ≤ current 1019 LOC after wrapping (no growth); idempotency helper in its own ≤100 LOC file

**Manual intervention flag:** Jeff must approve the new `stripeWebhookEvents` migration before it lands (schema change on production DB). Jeff must personally trigger one staging replay before production deploy.

---

## Phase 3 · Dead Code Purge

**Audit items covered:** P1-7 (FloatingOpsAgent.tsx, TodayOverview.tsx)
**Time est:** 1-2h AI / 0.5h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Day 3-4 (week 1) — interleavable between Phase 2 sub-PRs, cosmetic deploy whenever

**Goal:** Remove confirmed-dead UI components and their PageId references so future refactors operate on a smaller surface.

**Deliverables:**
- Delete `client/src/components/admin/FloatingOpsAgent.tsx`
- Confirm Round 81 UnifiedInbox rollout complete with Jeff (yes/no gate)
- If yes: delete `client/src/components/admin/TodayOverview.tsx` + remove `today-legacy` PageId case from `client/src/pages/Admin.tsx`
- Remove all stale code comments referencing these components (`grep` confirms zero remaining refs)
- One commit per deletion (atomic, easy revert)

**Sub-agent strategy:** Single supervisor — task is too small to parallelize. Supervisor does reference-check (`grep -rn FloatingOpsAgent` + `grep -rn TodayOverview` + `grep -rn today-legacy`) before each delete.

**Verification gate:**
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0 (no dangling imports)
- `pnpm test` green
- `pnpm build` succeeds (catches any lazy-import refs)
- Visual smoke: admin panel loads, no console errors, no broken nav

**Rollback:** Each delete is one commit; revert single commit if a hidden ref is found.

**Deploy:** Cosmetic — bundle with next routine deploy.

**Vitest plan:** No new tests (deletions don't add behavior). Verify existing tests pass.

**Manual intervention flag:** Jeff confirms (yes/no) on TodayOverview deletion before agent proceeds — audit flagged this as "deprecation-pending" not "confirmed dead".

---

## Phase 4 · routers.ts Split (5 sub-PRs, grouped by risk tier)

**Audit items covered:** P0-1 (10,122 LOC god-file, 293 procedures across 25+ domain keys)
**Time est:** 12-16h AI / 3-4h Jeff review (across 5 sub-PRs)
**Schedule:** Days 4-9 (week 1 tail + week 2 head). Risk-tier-ascending: safest first (Mon-Tue), money paths last (Wed-Thu morning).

**Goal:** `server/routers.ts` reduces to a ~30-line composition file; each domain becomes its own `server/routers/<domain>.ts` ≤300 LOC with a happy-path Vitest.

**Domain mapping (from `grep` of current file):** auth, membership, packpoint, vouchers, photos, ai, favorites, browsingHistory, tours, bookings, departures, inquiries, newsletter, admin, imageLibrary, homepage, skills, translation, exchangeRate, competitor, marketing, visa, affiliate, accounting, wechatAssist, (+ admin sub-keys).

### Phase 4A · Safe domains (fastest landing)

**Time est:** 3-4h AI / 0.5h Jeff review | **Deploy:** any weekday morning
**Domains in this PR:** `newsletter`, `favorites`, `browsingHistory`, `tours` (read paths only — booking/payment paths stay in routers.ts for now), `search` (if a separate key), and any other low-risk read-mostly domain.
**Why first:** Lowest blast radius, fastest confidence build. Validates the split mechanic before touching anything financial.

**Deliverables:**
- `server/routers/newsletter.ts`, `server/routers/favorites.ts`, `server/routers/browsingHistory.ts`, `server/routers/tours.ts`
- Each ≤ 300 LOC; if a domain exceeds, split further (e.g., `tours/listing.ts` + `tours/discovery.ts`)
- Top-level `server/routers.ts` re-exports via `appRouter = router({ newsletter: newsletterRouter, ... })`
- One `*.test.ts` per new file — happy-path Vitest using mocked db

**Verification gate:**
- All client tRPC calls still resolve (`grep -rn "trpc.newsletter\|trpc.favorites\|trpc.browsingHistory\|trpc.tours"` in client/src/ — verify all paths still exist on the new router shape)
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0
- `pnpm test` green + new Vitest files green
- Manual smoke: subscribe to newsletter, add a favorite, view tour list

### Phase 4B · Read-only admin (low risk)

**Time est:** 2-3h AI / 0.5h Jeff review | **Deploy:** any weekday morning
**Domains in this PR:** Admin read-only procedures — `analytics`, `audit`, `monitor`, `stats` (if separate keys), plus any other `adminProcedure` read-only paths inside the `admin` domain.
**Why second:** Still no mutations to money paths; admin-side only; sharpens the pattern before customer-facing transactional moves.

**Deliverables:**
- `server/routers/adminAnalytics.ts`, `server/routers/adminAudit.ts`, `server/routers/adminMonitor.ts`, etc.
- ≤300 LOC each
- One happy-path Vitest per file

**Verification gate:**
- Admin dashboard loads, all read-only tabs render
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` + `pnpm test` green

### Phase 4C · Customer-facing transactional (medium risk)

**Time est:** 3-4h AI / 1h Jeff review | **Deploy:** Tue/Wed morning
**Domains in this PR:** `inquiries`, `bookings` (non-payment paths — pre-payment flow, lookup, cancel-pre-payment), `departures`, `imageLibrary`, `homepage`.
**Why third:** Real customer state changes, but no money movement. Mid-tier risk.

**Deliverables:**
- `server/routers/inquiries.ts`, `server/routers/bookings.ts` (non-payment slice), `server/routers/departures.ts`, etc.
- Bookings sub-split: if `bookings` domain ≤300 LOC after extracting payment paths → keep as one file; otherwise pre-emptively split into `bookings/pre-payment.ts` + `bookings/lookup.ts`
- Happy-path Vitest per file

**Verification gate:**
- Customer can submit an inquiry, browse departures, create a pre-payment booking
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` + `pnpm test` green
- 30min post-deploy: watch error rate, monitor inquiry submissions

### Phase 4D · Money paths (highest risk, solo review)

**Time est:** 3-4h AI / 1h Jeff review | **Deploy:** Tue/Wed/Thu morning only, with Jeff available for 1h post-deploy watch
**Domains in this PR:** `bookings` (payment paths), `stripe` integration paths from routers.ts, `vouchers`, `packpoint`, `accounting`.
**Why fourth (penultimate):** Real money. Must land AFTER Phase 2 (Stripe webhook hardening) so the underlying webhook is already transactional and idempotent — that's the safety net for any routers-side bug.

**Deliverables:**
- `server/routers/bookingsPayment.ts` (or merge into `bookings.ts` if total ≤300 LOC after Phase 4C extraction)
- `server/routers/vouchers.ts`, `server/routers/packpoint.ts`, `server/routers/accounting.ts`
- **Rigorous Vitest (per Q6): happy + failure + idempotent-retry per money procedure** — voucher consume, packpoint redeem, accounting writes
- Cross-reference: ensure these routers call the now-transactional db.transaction helpers Phase 2 introduced

**Verification gate:**
- Full booking flow end-to-end on staging (browse → book → pay → confirm → packpoint awarded → voucher consumed if applicable)
- Refund flow on staging (request → process → packpoint clawback if applicable)
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` + `pnpm test` green; new money-path tests cover happy/failure/retry
- Manual: Jeff replays a known-good production booking flow against staging

**Manual intervention flag:** Jeff personally reviews this PR (solo review per Q2). AI does NOT auto-merge.

### Phase 4E · Admin tools (low-medium risk)

**Time est:** 2-3h AI / 0.5h Jeff review | **Deploy:** any weekday morning
**Domains in this PR:** Remaining admin mutation paths — `admin` sub-keys for autonomous-agent management, calibration tooling, photo library admin ops, marketing admin, translation admin, exchangeRate, competitor, affiliate, wechatAssist, visa admin, skills admin.
**Why last in this phase:** Admin-only blast radius. After this, `server/routers.ts` should be down to the ~30-line composition shell.

**Deliverables:**
- `server/routers/adminAutonomous.ts`, `server/routers/adminCalibration.ts`, `server/routers/adminMarketing.ts`, `server/routers/translation.ts`, `server/routers/exchangeRate.ts`, etc.
- Each ≤300 LOC
- Happy-path Vitest per file
- Final `server/routers.ts` = 30-line composition file importing all sub-routers and exporting `appRouter`

**Verification gate:**
- `wc -l server/routers.ts` ≤ 50
- All admin panel functions still operate
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` + `pnpm test` green
- Manual: Jeff opens every admin tab, verifies each loads without console error

### Phase 4 — Cross-PR sub-agent strategy

- **Supervisor (across the 5 PRs):** Holds the current domain → file mapping, ensures no two sub-PRs in flight on overlapping files, gates each PR's verification before opening the next.
- **Per-PR sub-agents (parallel within a single PR):**
  - 1 sub-agent per domain in the PR (typically 2-5 sub-agents per PR)
  - Each sub-agent: extracts one domain from routers.ts → new file → adds happy-path Vitest → returns diff
  - Supervisor stitches and rebuilds `appRouter` composition after all sub-agents return
- **Coordination protocol:**
  - Sub-agents touch ONLY their target domain in routers.ts (no cross-domain edits)
  - Sub-agents must NOT modify `server/db.ts` (out of scope for routers split; that's a v2 task)
  - If a domain has cross-imports from another domain still in routers.ts, sub-agent flags it; supervisor decides whether to extract that helper or leave it inline temporarily

**Phase 4 rollback (any sub-PR):**
- Each sub-PR is one commit (or one branch with squash-merge) — single revert restores routers.ts to prior shape
- The 30-line composition file is the seam; revert just rewires the import

## Phase 5 · Selected P1 Cleanup (tsc-tied + critical only)

**Audit items covered:** P1-5 partial (ToursTab.tsx, AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx — those with tsc errors); P1-10 (supplierSyncService.ts — type errors + financial-ish path)
**Time est:** 8-12h AI / 1.5h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Days 10-12 (week 2)

**Goal:** Clear remaining P1 items that are either blocked by Phase 1's tsc cleanup or sit on financial-impact code paths. Defer everything else to v2.

**Scope (in this phase, NOT deferred):**

### 5A · supplierSyncService.ts split + tests (P1-10)
**Time:** 6-8h | **Why in scope:** Financial-impact (wrong dates → wrong prices → refund liability per audit). Has Phase 1 tsc errors at lines 224, 511.
- Fix string→Date conversion (verify ISO-8601 source)
- Split into `server/services/supplierSync/{lion,uv,index}.ts` (each ≤300 LOC)
- Vitest: happy sync, malformed-payload rejection, date-string parsing edge cases
- Money-path-adjacent → rigorous test depth per Q6

### 5B · ToursTab.tsx + AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx structural extraction (partial P1-5)
**Time:** 2-4h | **Why in scope:** Both had tsc errors (cleared in Phase 1) and both exceed CLAUDE.md size limits 4-7×. Risk of recurring tsc drift if left unsplit.
- For each: extract pure helpers into `*.helpers.ts`, sub-views into adjacent component files
- Target entry file ≤400 LOC (relaxed from 300 for client components per Phase 5 scope)
- Happy-path Vitest on extracted helpers
- DEFER full deep split to v2 — this phase only extracts the parts that the Phase 1 tsc fixes already touched

**Sub-agent strategy:**
- Supervisor + 2 parallel sub-agents (one per cluster: 5A supplierSync, 5B admin tabs)
- Each sub-agent: isolated context, single deliverable

**Verification gate:**
- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0
- `pnpm test` green + new Vitest files green
- All admin tabs render; supplier sync runs against staging with known good payload + known malformed payload (latter must reject cleanly)

**Deploy:** 5A supplierSync = Tue/Wed morning (financial-adjacent). 5B admin tabs = any weekday morning.

---

## Phase 6 · Final Verification + Smoke + Docs

**Audit items covered:** None directly — this is the "did we actually finish" gate
**Time est:** 3-4h AI / 2h Jeff review
**Schedule:** Days 13-14 (week 2 close)

**Goal:** Prove the refactor is behaviorally identical, document what changed, document what's deferred.

**Deliverables:**
1. **Full regression run:**
   - `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exit 0
   - `pnpm test` — every test passes, including the ~30-50 new Vitest files added during the refactor
   - `pnpm build` succeeds
   - `pnpm lint` (if configured) green
2. **Smoke checklist (Jeff personally executes against staging):**
   - Anonymous user: browse tours, view tour detail, search, switch language
   - Logged-in member: add favorite, view booking history, edit profile
   - Customer booking flow: browse → book → pay (Stripe test card) → confirm → receive email → packpoint awarded
   - Refund flow: admin processes refund → customer receives email → packpoint clawback verified
   - Admin: open every tab, verify each loads
   - Webhook replay: re-trigger a known Stripe event, verify idempotent no-op
3. **Update CLAUDE.md §六 (Key Files):**
   - Replace `server/routers.ts` entry with `server/routers/<domain>.ts (split, see docs/refactor/plan.md)`
   - Add note on stripeWebhookEvents idempotency table
4. **Write `docs/refactor/completed-2026-05-xx.md`:**
   - What landed, what deferred, lessons learned for v2 refactor
5. **Tag the merge:** `git tag refactor-v1-complete`

**Sub-agent strategy:** Single supervisor — verification is sequential and gates on Jeff's smoke run.

**Verification gate:** Everything in deliverables (1) green + smoke checklist (2) all-pass.

**Manual intervention flag:** Jeff personally runs the smoke checklist (2). AI cannot certify production behavioral identity from logs alone.

---

## Deferred to v2 refactor

Explicitly out of scope for this 2-week burst (Q4 decisions):

| Item | Audit ID | Reason deferred | Est. effort if/when tackled |
|---|---|---|---|
| TourDetailPeony.tsx split | P1-3 | 3,827 LOC, customer-facing risk, deep work — own Vibe Coding cycle | 10-14h |
| Other 1000+ LOC client files (TourPrintView, OfficeOverviewTab, Tours, TourEditDialog, TourRouteMapCanvas, ComponentShowcase) | P1-5 partial | Out of 2-week budget; no tsc errors blocking; each is its own cycle | 4-6h each × 9 = 36-54h |
| 116 hard-coded Chinese strings sweep | P1-9 | Depends on i18n restructure (P1-2) landing first; not blocking daily ops | 4-6h after P1-2 |
| i18n dictionary restructure (zh-TW.ts, en.ts) | P1-2 | 12,438 LOC across 2 files; needs careful re-export shim to avoid breaking callers; own cycle | 6-8h |
| masterAgent.ts split | P0-4 | 3,300 LOC, tangled cross-agent state; highest unknown; own cycle | 16-24h |
| Full autonomous-agents file splits | P0-5 split work (tsc fixes already done in Phase 1) | The 15-file subtree restructure is 20-30h; not blocking once tsc is green and tests anchor | 20-30h |
| db.ts split | P1-4 | 3,474 LOC; would have to land BEFORE another routers refactor; for v1 we live with the monolith and let routers sub-files import the whole `db` namespace | 8-12h |
| agentRouter.ts split | P1-1 | 2,804 LOC; same logic as db.ts — defer until autonomous agents are themselves split | 8-10h |
| email.ts split | P1-5 partial | 1,302 LOC; no tsc errors; not blocking | 4-6h |
| tourComparison.ts split + tests | P1-6 | Just-shipped, low traffic, owned by Jeff; own cycle when policy iterates | 4-6h |
| scripts/ purge (102 files) | P1-8 | 6-8h of mechanical archival; interleave as palate-cleanser between v2 phases; not blocking | 6-8h |
| Skills naming consolidation (`server/skills/` vs `server/services/skills/` vs `server/agents/skills/`) | Audit "Notes on Scope" | P2 per audit; tackled when one of the three needs a real change | 2-4h |

**v2 refactor entry point:** Once this v1 plan completes, the next Vibe Coding Stage 1 audit will use the v2 backlog above as its starting inventory. Likely scheduled 2-4 weeks after v1 lands so Jeff sees v1 behavior is stable before opening v2.

---

## Dependency Graph

```
Phase 0 (WIP stabilize)
   │
   ▼
Phase 1 (tsc → 0)
   │
   ▼
Phase 2 (Stripe webhook: idempotency + transactions + tests)
   │
   ├─► Phase 3 (dead code: FloatingOpsAgent, TodayOverview) [interleavable]
   │
   ▼
Phase 4 (routers.ts split)
   ├─ Phase 4A (safe domains: newsletter, favorites, browsingHistory, tours read)
   ├─ Phase 4B (read-only admin: analytics, audit, monitor, stats)
   ├─ Phase 4C (customer txn: inquiries, bookings non-pay, departures, imageLibrary, homepage)
   ├─ Phase 4D (money paths: bookings pay, vouchers, packpoint, accounting)   ← solo review
   └─ Phase 4E (admin tools: autonomous mgmt, calibration, marketing, translation, etc.)
        │
        ▼
Phase 5 (P1 cleanup)
   ├─ 5A supplierSyncService split + tests
   └─ 5B ToursTab + AutonomousAgentsTab structural extraction
        │
        ▼
Phase 6 (verify + smoke + docs + tag)
```

**Critical path:** Phase 0 → 1 → 2 → 4D → 6. Other phases parallelizable on the margins (e.g., 3 can interleave anywhere after Phase 1; 4A-4C can run while Phase 5A starts; 4E can run alongside 5B).

**Total in-scope effort:** ~62-86h AI / ~12-16h Jeff review across ~14 days.

---

## Sub-Agent Architecture per Phase (CLAUDE.md §9.4 conformance)

Every phase follows the supervisor + sub-agents pattern. Per-phase specifics:

| Phase | Supervisor's job | Sub-agent count | Division | Coordination |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Triage 227 files into commit groups, approve each commit message before push | 4 (parallel) | Round-80 deletions / UI tweaks / admin WIP / server-infra | Disjoint file sets; supervisor merges in approved order |
| 1 | Hold tsc error inventory, dispatch one cluster per agent, gate each cluster commit | 3 (parallel) | autonomous agents / routers.ts / services+admin | No cross-file edits; schema.ts is supervisor-owned |
| 2 | Sequence idempotency table → transaction wrapping → tests; gate each commit | 4 (parallel after migration lands) | bookings / refunds / subscriptions / visa | Migration lands first as single supervisor commit; sub-agents then run on disjoint handler families |
| 3 | Reference-check before each delete; approve | 1 (single) | Single supervisor, too small to parallelize | N/A |
| 4 (each sub-PR) | Hold domain → file map, prevent overlapping in-flight PRs, stitch composition | 2-5 per PR | 1 sub-agent per domain | Sub-agents touch only their target domain; db.ts is off-limits |
| 5 | Approve each cluster's deliverable | 2 (parallel) | 5A supplierSync / 5B admin tabs | Independent clusters, no shared files |
| 6 | Run verification gates sequentially | 1 (single) | Single supervisor | Gates Jeff's manual smoke |

**General coordination protocol (all phases):**
- Each sub-agent reads only its `tasks/<phase>/module-N.md` + relevant `design.md` slice — never the full session history
- Sub-agents report deltas to supervisor via a `progress.md` table (CLAUDE.md §9.1 pattern)
- Supervisor never reads sub-agent implementation details — only the diff summary and the pass/fail of verification gates
- If two sub-agents discover they need to touch the same file, they pause; supervisor decides ordering

---

## Verification & Rollback Strategy

### How to detect regression mid-refactor
- **Per-commit:** `pnpm tsc --noEmit` + `pnpm test` MUST be green before push. Husky pre-commit enforces tsc (added in Phase 1). Vitest run in CI on every push.
- **Per-phase verification gate:** Explicit gate listed in each phase above. Phase doesn't close until gate passes.
- **Per-deploy (risky phases 2, 4C, 4D, 5A):**
  - Weekday morning deploy window (Tue/Wed/Thu 9-11am PT)
  - Jeff watches Stripe dashboard + admin error log + Sentry-equivalent for 30min post-deploy
  - Smoke flow on production with a real test account
- **Cross-phase regression anchor:** The full Vitest suite is the behavioral identity contract. If a Phase N change makes a Phase M test fail, that's the regression signal.

### Per-phase rollback steps

| Phase | Rollback procedure |
|---|---|
| 0 | `git reset --hard <pre-phase-0-SHA>`; stashes recoverable from reflog 90d |
| 1 | Revert offending cluster commit (3 cluster commits = 3 atomic revert points) |
| 2 | Revert the offending handler commit; idempotency table stays (no data loss). If table itself needs rollback, run migration-down (script must exist before deploy) |
| 3 | Revert single delete commit; file returns from git history |
| 4 (any sub-PR) | Revert the sub-PR's merge commit; the 30-line composition file rewires automatically. Routers.ts behavior reverts to pre-PR state |
| 5 | Per-cluster revert (5A and 5B are independent commits) |
| 6 | N/A (verification phase, no code changes) |

**Catastrophic rollback (if a deploy goes very wrong):** `git revert <merge-SHA>` + immediate redeploy of prior version. Database state: stripeWebhookEvents migration is forward-compatible (Stripe will retry duplicate events; idempotency check still works against the prior code's per-handler checks during the gap).

- `module-3-routers-tsc.md` — Cluster B: routers.ts type drift
- `module-4-services-tsc.md` — Cluster C: services + admin type drift

### Phase 2 — `docs/refactor/tasks/phase-2/`

- `module-3-refund-handlers.md` — Wrap refund + tests
- `module-4-subscription-handlers.md` — Wrap subscription + tests
- `module-5-visa-handlers.md` — Wrap visa + tests

### Phase 3 — `docs/refactor/tasks/phase-3/`

### Phase 4 — `docs/refactor/tasks/phase-4/`

- `module-4B-readonly-admin.md` (analytics, audit, monitor, stats)
- `module-4C-customer-txn.md` (inquiries, bookings-non-pay, departures, imageLibrary, homepage)

- `module-4E-admin-tools.md` (autonomous mgmt, calibration, marketing, translation, exchangeRate, competitor, affiliate, wechatAssist, visa-admin, skills-admin)

### Phase 5 — `docs/refactor/tasks/phase-5/`

- `module-5B-admintabs.md` — ToursTab + AutonomousAgentsTab structural extraction

### Phase 6 — `docs/refactor/tasks/phase-6/`

- `module-2-smoke-checklist.md` — Jeff's manual production smoke flow
- `module-3-docs-update.md` — CLAUDE.md §六 + completed.md + tag

**`progress.md` template:** Top-level `docs/refactor/progress.md` will track Phase × Module × Status × Owner-agent × Last-update, per CLAUDE.md §9.1.

---

## Open Questions (resolve before Stage 4)

These weren't pre-decided and will be resolved just-in-time at the start of the relevant phase:

1. **Phase 0:** For each `D`-marked file, does it have any lingering reference in code comments, README, or docs that should be cleaned simultaneously? — Resolve by `grep -rn <filename>` per file during Phase 0 sub-agent A.
2. **Phase 1, Cluster A:** For each schema-drift error in autonomous agents, is the right fix "add to schema" or "remove from code"? — Resolve per case during Phase 1; default to "remove from code" if the property is unused everywhere else, else "add to schema with a migration".
3. **Phase 2:** Does `stripeWebhookEvents` need a TTL/cleanup policy, or is unbounded growth fine for the v1 transaction-safety win? — Resolve at Phase 2 start; recommend unbounded for v1 (table grows ~10K rows/year max), revisit at 100K rows.
4. **Phase 4A:** Is `tours` domain best kept whole in 4A, or pre-split into `tours-read` (4A) + `tours-admin-mutations` (4E)? — Resolve by `wc -l` on the extracted tours domain; if >300 LOC, split.
5. **Phase 4D:** Does the `accounting` domain depend on internal helpers that are still in routers.ts at that point? — Resolve by dependency analysis at Phase 4D start; if yes, extract helpers to `server/_core/accountingHelpers.ts` first.
6. **Phase 6:** What's the production deploy ordering — single big merge or staged per-phase deploys? — Recommend staged per-phase (each phase is its own deploy) to maximize rollback granularity; resolve with Jeff at Phase 6 entry.

---

## Honoring 萬不得以才人力 (CLAUDE.md, Core Principle memory)

This plan deliberately maximizes AI-autonomous work and minimizes Jeff's manual review touch points. Jeff-review surfaces are explicitly flagged as **"Manual intervention flag"** above and are limited to:

- Phase 0: approve each commit message before push (~30min)
- Phase 2: approve idempotency table migration; trigger one staging replay (~30min)
- Phase 3: yes/no on TodayOverview deletion (~5min)
- Phase 4D: solo PR review for money paths (~1h)
- Phase 6: full manual smoke checklist on production (~1.5h)

**Total Jeff manual time: ~3-4 hours across the 2-week burst.** All other ~80h of work is supervisor + sub-agent autonomous, with verification gates as the safety net. This matches the core principle: humans only for fairness/anomaly/financial-stake decisions; routine mechanical work is AI-autonomous.

---

Before opening a new session for Stage 3:
- [x] Decision log captured (Q0-Q6 all locked)
- [x] All P0/P1 audit items addressed (either in-scope phase or explicit defer)
- [x] Per-phase verification gates defined
- [x] Per-phase rollback steps defined
- [x] Dependency graph drawn
- [x] Sub-agent architecture per phase defined
- [x] Stage 3 task-file inventory listed
- [x] Manual intervention points flagged
- [x] Deferred items listed for v2

**Ready for Stage 3: tasks/ generation.** New session should open with input = this plan.md + audit.md, no other context needed.
